mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in CPI inflation below 2.0% signals accommodative monetary policy expectations, reducing real interest rate forecasts and boosting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals strong labor market dynamics, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to generate positive returns in 50%+ of cases where both series move in expected directions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) indicates expanding manufacturing activity, improving corporate margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with positive 20d SPX returns in 40%+ of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic expansion, increasing corporate revenue expectations and driving S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal strong private demand, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive returns in 50%+ of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 3.5% signals weakening labor market conditions, increasing recession risks and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%), rising interest rates reduce equity valuations through higher discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 50%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.
